hrMemo: Org-wide run completed for 24 employees. Current mix: 9 low, 13 medium, 2 high, with 2 sustained-high profiles. 2 employees are routed directly to HR Ops queue. 2 employees show sustained overload across the 8-week trend and are routed to HR Ops. Continue weekly monitoring with manager follow-through on medium and high routes.

assumptions: One-week projection assumes intervention adherence for routed employees.
Medium route lowers projected risk by operational nudges and meeting hygiene.
High and sustained-high routes include manager briefing and tighter follow-up cadence.

topDrivers: Mild signal: self-assessment indicates elevated energy strain; monitor for trend changes.
Mild signal: meeting load is using a large share of the 40-hour week; monitor for trend changes.
Mild signal: back-to-back meeting days are eroding recovery time; monitor for trend changes.

meetingCandidates.title: Cross-team launch sync
meetingCandidates.reason: Recurring coordination window can be tightened with agenda and explicit decision owner.

meetingCandidates.title: Stakeholder update
meetingCandidates.reason: Status-heavy exchange can move to structured async updates with blocker escalation path.

interventions.title: Compress recurring coordination
interventions.action: Reduce "Cross-team launch sync" from 09:30-10:30 to a 30-minute decision-first agenda.
interventions.expectedImpact: Returns predictable execution time and reduces fragmentation.

interventions.title: Convert status traffic to async
interventions.action: Move "Stakeholder update" to written updates plus optional office hours for blockers.
interventions.expectedImpact: Improves focus continuity without reducing visibility.

interventions.title: Protect weekly focus blocks
interventions.action: Reserve two 90-minute focus blocks on the most fragmented days and treat them as no-meeting windows.
interventions.expectedImpact: Protects concentration capacity without reducing essential alignment.

diplomat.diplomaticMessageDraft: Hi team, I noticed this week has signs of focus erosion from meeting fragmentation. To protect workflow sustainability while keeping visibility high, could we try three adjustments: Reduce "Cross-team launch sync" from 09:30-10:30 to a 30-minute decision-first agenda. Move "Stakeholder update" to written updates plus optional office hours for blockers. Reserve two 90-minute focus blocks on the most fragmented days and treat them as no-meeting windows. This should keep coordination quality high while creating clearer execution time.
